name: Matcha Powdered Green Tea Japan
desc: High-grade shaded green tea ground into a fine powder. The heart of the traditional tea ceremony culture in Japan.

name: Sencha Loose Leaf Green Tea Japan
desc: The most popular everyday tea, steamed and rolled into needle-like shapes. Represents the most common tea crop in Japan.

name: Hojicha Roasted Green Tea Japan
desc: Tea leaves roasted in a porcelain pot over charcoal, giving it a distinct reddish-brown color and toasty aroma in Japan.

name: Genmaicha Brown Rice Tea Japan
desc: Green tea mixed with roasted popped brown rice. Often called 'popcorn tea' and loved for its nutty flavor across Japan.

name: Gyokuro Shaded Premium Tea Japan
desc: One of the most expensive varieties, grown under shade for weeks to increase theffanine and umami content in Japan.

name: Kukicha Twig Tea Japan
desc: A unique tea made from the stems, stalks, and twigs of the tea plant. Known for its low caffeine and creamy taste in Japan.

name: Mugicha Barley Tea Japan
desc: A caffeine-free roasted grain tea typically served cold. The quintessential refreshing drink for the hot summers in Japan.

name: Bancha Harvest Green Tea Japan
desc: Harvested from the later flushes of the season. Known as a robust, standard-grade tea served in many restaurants in Japan.

name: Sakuracha Cherry Blossom Tea Japan
desc: Pickled cherry blossoms steeped in hot water. A beautiful, salty-sweet floral tea served at weddings and celebrations in Japan.

name: Yuzucha Citrus Tea Japan
desc: A soothing herbal tea made from the aromatic Yuzu citrus fruit and honey, popular during the winter solstice in Japan.

name: Chawan Tea Ceremony Bowl Japan
desc: The ceramic vessel used for preparing and drinking Matcha. An essential piece of traditional artisan pottery in Japan.

name: Kyusu Teapot Infuser Japan
desc: The classic side-handle teapot designed specifically for brewing loose-leaf green teas like Sencha in Japan.
